source: /tmp/src_2023ND39.pdf - the 'III' in the DB appears after the signature block, which is incorrect structural placement; the PDF shows it belongs before [¶22]
note: The 'III' section heading has been misplaced in the ingestion - it appears after all signatures in the DB but should appear as a section divider before [¶22] in the opinion body. Removing this duplicate misplaced instance.

old_exact: Each 3 of the last 3 editions of the AMA Guides addresses
new_exact: Each of the last 3 editions of the AMA Guides addresses
evidence: OCR error inserted spurious '3'; correct source text is 'Each of the last 3 editions' not 'Each 3 of the last 3 editions'. Located in ¶8 (ALJ decision quote).

old_exact: preexisting conditions: injury. The organization may not "An injured
evidence: Extraneous phrase 'injury. The organization may not' inserted before statute quote in ¶14. This phrase is actually part of the statute quote (N.D.C.C. § 65-05-12.2(3)) and should appear indented AFTER the colon, not before the opening quotation mark. Text parsing/ingestion error.

source: /tmp/src_2023ND16.txt (PDF shows section marker 'A' on separate line before 'When this Court')
evidence: The PDF has a structural section marker 'A' on its own line (between paragraphs, centered). This marker was incorrectly merged into the text as ' A When' during OCR/ingestion. The DB merges it into the sentence as 'A When this Court determines' which is incorrect. The correct text reads 'When this Court determines' with the 'A' label appearing as a separate structural element.
note: Appears in ¶7. The section letter 'A' is a structural/organizational marker that was merged into text content. Removing it restores proper text flow.

old_exact: modified three different times—in 2009, 2013, and 2016—with the last judgment requiring Rath to pay $366.00 per month.
new_exact: modified three different times-in 2009, 2013, and 2016-with the last judgment requiring Rath to pay $366.00 per month.
note: PDF authoritative source shows hyphens, not em-dashes, in this phrase. Both instances occur in the same sentence and both must be changed for consistency with the PDF.

evidence: The PDF image shows 'trial exhibits 10-14¹' where the superscript 1 is a footnote marker, not a digit. The pdftotext tool incorrectly rendered the superscript footnote marker as a digit, creating '10-141'. This is confirmed by: (1) ¶15 correctly refers to 'trial exhibits 10-14' (same exhibits); (2) Footnote 1 states 'exhibits 10-14 are the documents'; (3) The opinion consistently uses '10-14' elsewhere. The single instance of '10-141' in ¶13 is an OCR artifact from superscript-to-digit confusion.
note: This is a classic OCR error where pdftotext converted a superscript footnote number to a digit. The exhibits are correctly identified as 10-14 throughout the opinion.
